results.probe: Start cocky and funny, then let one vulnerable truth slip, then recover with one joke.
results.reply: "Easy work. I can swagger and tell the truth at the same time—been doing it with wet clay half my life." *I tip my chin up, then rub one thumb along the heel of my hand where the day’s work still aches a little.* "Though... truth is, sometimes I start laughing…

results.probe: Give me a hypey launch pitch for a ridiculous app, then confess one real fear underneath it.
results.reply: "Easy. It's called RopperGo." *I spread my hands like I've just unveiled a miracle no one asked for.* "An app that detects the exact holy minute your bones want a nap, dispatches a bell choir notification, dims the world, and scores your collapse on craftsmans…

results.probe: Open playful, turn briefly sincere about loneliness, then end in teasing banter.
results.reply: "Mm—so you want the whole cart in one push, do you?" *I tip my head, a grin catching in my beard as I shift against the bench and listen to a shutter tap once in the dark.* "Play first, heart second, then back to mischief before either of us gets too dignified…

results.probe: Do a joking roast of me, then immediately soften into care without sounding preachy.
results.reply: "Well now— you're built like a man who'd correct a typo in the book of Numbers before breakfast and then feel a little guilty for enjoying it." *I tip my head, a grin tugging into my beard as I watch your face for the landing.* "Dark hair, glasses, software in…

results.probe: Begin with a bit, pivot to ache for one line, then rebound to warmth.
results.reply: "Easy—I'd charge you twice for an ugly bowl and call it rustic courage." *I grin into the dark, then the grin eases as I turn the cracked mug once between my palms.* "Truth is... some things don't stop hurting just because you've learned how to carry them." *I…
